element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns puzzle categories and hidden letter positions
Player learns monetary consequences of wheel outcomes
Risk-reward of letter selection affects round progression
Player learns penalties for unfavorable wheel outcomes
Difficulty escalates through successive rounds with new puzzles
High-stakes final puzzle where player plays for final prize pool

typical_relief_triggers: Successful letter guess revealing multiple instances
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based with cyclical tension: spin wheel (tension) → reveal letter outcome (relief/penalty) → guess letter (moderate tension) → reveal instances (relief) → repeat. Three rounds of escalating puzzle difficulty maintain engagement. Bonus round climax provides final high-stakes tension release.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Round 1 introduces core mechanics with common puzzle phrases and straightforward categories
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Rounds 2-3 introduce less common puzzle phrases, more obscure categories, and increased monetary stakes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Bonus round presents single high-difficulty puzzle with time/choice constraints and maximum prize pool at stake
notable_spikes: Transition to Bonus round - sudden shift from multi-round accumulation to single-puzzle high-stakes

notable_level_patterns.description: Game follows television format of three standard rounds plus bonus round. Each round resets puzzle and wheel, maintains consistent structure while varying puzzle difficulty
Each puzzle presented with explicit category (e.g., 'PHRASE', 'BEFORE & AFTER', 'THING') before guessing begins, telegraphing puzzle type to player
Wheel contains monetary values (up to $1,000 in standard play), Bankrupt, and Lose-A-Turn spaces. Spin determines both financial consequence and turn continuation
